note: Generated by scripts/roadmap-to-json.js. Do not edit by hand; edit ROADMAP.md and regenerate.

now.text: Multiplayer co-presence + the character selector. Co-presence CLIENT WIRING SHIPPED (v0.472): two players share the VPS world, stream position, and see each other as avatars; pending a two-player test. Remaining: nameplates, the world-snapshot prefill, and the CHARACTER LAUNCHER (the Play button becomes a launcher with character select + homes + a default to skip it; self-custodial LOCAL vs server-authoritative SERVER characters, open / closed / hybrid like Diablo II). Design in docs/design/characters-and-servers.md.

now.version: v0.473: the banks now charge/discharge with the solar swing, live HUD readout
now.text: First Playable / live home sim depth. Battery state-of-charge SHIPPED (v0.473: the banks now charge/discharge with the solar swing, live HUD readout). Remaining: walk-up stations, a 3D vitals HUD, death and respawn, a guided first day, and letting battery discharge prevent load-shedding.
GitHub branch + tag protection on main (deploy auto-pushes to the live relay with no approval gate) and the backup-restore drill.

themes.title: Survival and self-sufficiency
themes.description: The heart of the mission: in-game systems that map to real-world systems a person can

items.text: Crafting loop, mine to refine to craft, with a 2-tier ore to ingot to alloy chain.
Cooking, nutrition, and vitals: eat, decay, conditions, well-fed.
Gardening: plant, water, harvest, with a Garden panel.
Drone and asteroid mining: commission, trip, mine a finite asteroid, deliver.
Skills and XP, with tech-unlock gating and a quest chain.
Aeroponic towers end to end: curated configs, a compatibility check, a 3D placeholder, the inventory tree, plant to grow to harvest, and a real-world parts list.
Seed economy: survival mode consumes seeds, a starter grant, and a harvest that returns seeds so the loop sustains itself.
Homestead designs: the Fibonacci self-sufficient blueprint as a browsable design, plus a self-sufficiency model (coupled energy / water / food / waste loops).
Save and load: offline progress (inventory and skills) now persists between sessions.
Garden plot-types registry: generalize "tower" into a data-driven growing method (aeroponic / soil / sand / pot / raised-bed / direct-sow / trays), each moddable, none hardcoded.
First Playable arc: persistence depth, a 3D vitals HUD, walk-up stations, death and respawn, and a guided first day.

Real-hardware control layer: bind a home to real monitoring and automation hardware (for example real aeroponics, solar, water), the north star where the game becomes the control panel for your actual homestead.

Open sensor protocol: a simple JSON-over-HTTP spec so a 5-dollar ESP32 feeds your real pH / EC / temperature / water readings into the live home sim, the exact moment the game becomes the control panel for your actual homestead.
Disaster Mode: aim the disaster / weather / hydrology / atmosphere systems at real preparedness, a location-personalized prep plan computed from the codex and your battery days-of-autonomy.
Parts-list-to-real: for every buildable system, a refined bill of materials you can 3D-print, buy, or trade for.

themes.title: Identity and privacy
themes.description: You own your identity and your data. No accounts, no passwords, no tracking. Even the

items.text: Post-quantum identity: a Dilithium3 / ML-DSA-65 key derived from a BIP39 24-word seed (which is also your Solana wallet). The canonical crypto details live in the Cryptography section of CLAUDE.md.
End-to-end encrypted DMs: pure Kyber768 / ML-KEM-768 to BLAKE3-KDF to AES-256-GCM. The relay stores only ciphertext.
Encrypted vault (PBKDF2 600k both clients) with three auto-unlock modes.
BIP39 24-word recovery and social recovery, so losing a device never locks you out forever.
Proof-of-possession at connect: a signed challenge-response before the relay binds your identity (closes identity spoofing).
Release signing: a hybrid Ed25519 + Dilithium3 signature on every release (both must verify), so a compromised GitHub or a stray tag can never push code to your machine.
Member-directory opt-out: a privacy setting so you can join a server without appearing in its public member list, with a user-facing toggle.

Self-custodial vs server-authoritative characters: your identity and look are always yours; a server holds your in-world progression only when you choose its closed realm (open vs closed Battle.net). Designed in docs/design/characters-and-servers.md.
Multi-device key sync: authorize a second device by QR or short code over an encrypted channel (part of the device mesh).
Skill Passport: a portable, offline-verifiable, post-quantum proof of competence (W3C-style credential, but federated and PQ), direct infrastructure for the unbanked and undocumented.
STARK selective disclosure: prove a fact about yourself without revealing the underlying data.

themes.title: Communication and federation
themes.description: Talk, meet, and organize without a gatekeeper. Servers are meeting places, not owners

items.text: Chat: channels, threaded replies, reactions, pins, search, and direct messages.
Voice and video calling on the web (WebRTC peer-to-peer with screen share).
Peer-to-peer groups: signed objects, end-to-end encryption, and a group that behaves like a channel, on both web and native (Phases 1 and 2).
Signed profile replication: your profile gossips between servers, so there is no single home server to lose.
Web-to-native parity: rebuilding the web chat view to mirror the native app one-to-one (Track W).
Federation activation: an admin UI to add and trust peer servers, a second operator-run relay, end-to-end federation testing, then vetted third-party peers.
Peer-to-peer groups Phases 3 to 5: relay-independent transport (a group survives even if its home relay dies) and serverless discovery.

Native voice: mic capture, Opus over WebRTC, and per-channel voice rooms that interoperate with the web client for live two-way audio, with RNNoise noise suppression and selectable transmit modes (open mic, push-to-talk, voice-activated, push-to-mute).
Native voice polish: per-peer volume and mute controls, web transmit-mode UI parity, an in-process WebRTC test harness for CI, and graceful relay restart so deploys do not drop active calls.
Native streaming and video: the screen-share and video transport native still lacks (web users have video today).
Calendar with RSVP: events, recurring schedules, group calendars, reminders.
Mobile clients for Android and iOS.

themes.title: Governance and economy
themes.description: Turn groups into cooperatives. Coordinate work, trade fairly, and govern by a

items.text: The Humanity Accord: a CC0 constitution that binds the platform, readable in-app.
Tasks and mission control: a Fibonacci-scoped board for coordinating work at every scale.
Marketplace: listings, images, reviews, and seller ratings.
Reputation: peer-endorsed skills signed with your identity key.
Group governance: proposals, ranked-choice voting, and quorum rules, with every vote signed and tamper-evident.
Native trade UI completion (the page exists; the trade events need wiring).
Learning paths: complete a module, get peer-endorsed, unlock the next.
Time-Bank: log and exchange hours of help as the on-ramp economy before anyone has money, so the poorest can participate from day one with skills and time.

Build-It-For-Real: a structured bill of materials with local sourcing and a neighbor group-buy button on every survival system, because poverty is often a minimum-order-quantity problem, not a knowledge one.
Mission Pulse: a federated, privacy-preserving, aggregate-only impact dashboard (each server publishes signed counts, no per-user data leaves home).
AI agent governance: evolve Article 14 of the Accord from documented intent into enforced rules with appeals as more AI participants join.

themes.title: The simulation
themes.description: A 3D educational world where you learn real survival and production skills by doing.

items.text: Engine: a wgpu PBR renderer, an ECS, and 40-plus game systems wired in.
World: icosphere planets with level-of-detail, voxel asteroids, and a ship-at-origin starting world.
The full production, survival, and progression sandbox (the gameplay-loop arc, v0.329 to v0.342).
UI consolidation: a slim top navigation, universal reusable widgets, the Humanity Mission Dashboard, and the in-app Library.

In-app construction editor: build the homestead from inside the app: a top-down plan, a 3D astral camera + room grab, a three-column editor, doors and windows as placed objects you add / move / resize on still-solid walls, and multistory with level-aware adjacency so stacked rooms never cut doors into floors.
Structural de-risk spike: a pure node-beam solver (load routing, cascade failure, disconnected-island detection) so the structural-integrity pass is proven before it is wired to geometry.

Construction editor v2, the homestead-builder rebuild (v0.532 to v0.603): a fixed outer box plus freely-placed interior walls (rooms emerge by flood-fill), data-driven wall materials / thickness / surface layers, mitred corners, doors and windows with animated styles (swing / slide / iris / rotate / fold / energy / nanowall / fixed) plus locks and control panels, per-home lights, a unified single-line object browser, move / duplicate gizmos, undo / redo, a CAD dimension overlay, a construction console (the AI act surface) + live JSON home introspection (the AI read surface), and first-person wall / door collision.

Structural pieces (v0.583 to v0.592): a data-driven registry of buildable stairs, ramps, ladders (climb), elevators (ride), teleporters, train rail, decks, and roads as a curved node graph; walk on them in first person.

Home power sim + buildability (v0.437 to v0.606): machines are live ECS power entities (generators / consumers / batteries) that load-shed by priority and charge / discharge with the solar swing; a design-time buildability validator (power source, energy balance, wiring, conduits, power circuit).

Utility wiring, no magic transmission (v0.604 to v0.608): power, water, air, and data travel through rated cables and pipes (real AWG / ampacity / voltage-drop physics); machines declare IN / OUT ports by utility. SHIPPED: the data model + cable registry + physics, machine ports + the Conduits buildability check, the Power-circuit connectivity check, runtime per-island power-flow gating, and a live water / plumbing sim coupled to power (powered pumps fill the cistern; cut the power and it drains). Next: a wire-A-to-B gizmo and the room-temperature superconductor upgrade mission. Design in docs/design/utility-wiring.md.

Multiplayer co-presence + the character / server model: two players in one world on the VPS; self-custodial local characters vs server-authoritative ones (open vs closed Battle.net); the Play launcher with character select, homes, and a default.
First Playable: walk-up stations, a 3D vitals HUD, and a guided first day (shared with the survival theme above).
Generation-Ship co-op: a shared life-support habitat where selfishness literally collapses the colony, the first mission-shaped multiplayer scenario.
AI Mentor "Sage": a first-class NPC citizen with its own identity and a scripted-fallback decision tree (ships with zero LLM dependency, pluggable later).
Game-world depth: more content, characters, and deeper coupled systems.
Real-terrain world generation from real elevation data (USGS / SRTM / Copernicus) keyed to a place's latitude and longitude.
VR support and a "boot straight into Play" mode.

themes.title: Accessibility and onboarding
themes.description: The mission requires that anyone, of any age or background or ability, can use this.

items.text: Accessibility modes: high-contrast, colorblind, and reduced-motion (now wired and applying on every WEB page too, not only native, with the Settings toggles actually taking effect, v0.471).
Glossary overlay on the web (150-plus plain-language term definitions).
Localization in 5 languages (English, Spanish, French, Japanese, Chinese).
The Mission Dashboard: makes "what I am doing" and "what we are doing together" instantly clear to anyone.
Three-audience onboarding: clear, separate getting-started guides for standard users, server admins, and AI agents (this refactor).
A tooltip on every interactive element, in plain language.
A "first 5 minutes" guided tour for brand-new users.
Localization expansion: Arabic, Hindi, Portuguese, Russian, German, Swahili, and more.
A native glossary widget to match the web.
A full WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility audit with fixes.

Schema-driven in-app "Make-a-Thing" editor: a form generated from each data schema so a teacher anywhere can add local crops, recipes, and lessons with no code (the keystone for community content and against founder-dependence).
Offline Survival Codex: the chemistry / biome / water-and-nutrient corpus bundled, printable, and with built-in calculators that work with no internet.

themes.title: Infrastructure and sovereignty
themes.description: The platform must survive disasters, censorship, and the maintainer stepping away.

items.text: One unified binary: full desktop app, or headless relay server, from the same code.
Resilience: off-site 3-2-1 backups, SQLite corruption recovery, a liveness watchdog with self-heal, and multi-channel alerting.
Abuse resistance: fail2ban, per-key rate limits, signed-request anti-replay, and per-author submission quotas (the security sprint, v0.420 to v0.422).
Distribution: GitHub plus a self-hosted Forgejo mirror plus a BitTorrent seeder.

Release-signing pipeline verified end to end (a release dual-signed Ed25519 + Dilithium3, the fail-closed updater offering it) plus a check-signing health gate so an unsigned latest release can never again silently freeze auto-update.
First dependency security audit (cargo audit on the cadence): three TLS certificate-validation advisories patched via a rustls-webpki upgrade.
In-app ops console: bring every admin action into the app (the GUI-first mandate), paying down the CLI-only debt.
Device mesh: a "My Devices" dashboard, backup designation, restore flow, and direct LAN sync, so your own devices back each other up.
Litestream continuous replication (roughly 1-minute recovery point) and a quarterly backup-restore drill.
GitHub branch + tag protection and required signed commits on main (deploy auto-pushes to the live relay today with no approval gate).
FederationBundle: one signed-object bundle that travels over any medium (USB, QR, LoRa, sneakernet), so identity and knowledge move with no internet at all.
Continuity Capsule: self-sovereign succession via a Shamir-split seed across trusted contacts, for every citizen, not just the operator.
LoRa mesh radio integration (needs hardware on hand).
Distribution beyond GitHub: Codeberg, Software Heritage, a WinGet manifest, and IPFS.

recent.text: Telecom consequence: a powered WiFi router's RF now HARMS the grow -- the FarmingSystem drains crop health by the home RF level, so you run wired (Cat6/fibre, zero RF) to protect a sensitive crop. The operator's "the tradeoffs bite" example.

Telecom / internet utility Stage 1: real data media (Cat6 ethernet, fibre, WiFi) with bandwidth / range / latency / RF-emission tradeoffs + a link-physics check. Next: emissions become enemy/player detection signatures + pheromones.

Air life-support Stage 2: occupancy + a powered air recycler -- cut the power and the scrubbers stop, O2 falls, and the player suffocates (power -> air -> Vitals). All three life-support utilities (energy, water, air) now have real consequences.

Build-editor backlog (multi-select, snap guides, lock-per-type), the cable picker + superconductor bulk-upgrade, a CI-lint fix, and AIR life-support Stage 1 (the AtmosphereSystem is live: a sealed home air space + a Live air Home-page card).

Water to food: the FarmingSystem reads the live cistern level -- a dry cistern stops auto-irrigation and the garden wilts, completing power to water to food (cut the power, the pump stops, the cistern drains, then days later the crops die).
Water sim fix (adversarial review): the seed home now actually fills when powered + drains when cut (the pump was over-modelled; the towers were inert islands).

Live water / plumbing sim coupled to power: cisterns store, powered pumps + purifiers fill them, fixtures draw, all per pipe island -- and cutting the power stops the water (the first power -> water consequence chain). Shown live on the Home page.

Utility wiring: real copper-cable physics + a conduit registry, machine IN / OUT ports, the Conduits + Power-circuit buildability checks, a physically wired seed home, and runtime per-island power-flow gating (no magic transmission).

Structural pieces (stairs, ramps, ladders, elevators, teleporters, train rail, decks, roads as a curved node graph) plus build-editor polish: a unified object browser, move / duplicate gizmos, a wall wireframe + dimension overlay.

The home-construction rebuild: a fixed box plus interior walls (rooms emerge by flood-fill), wall materials / thickness / layers, mitred corners, doors / windows / locks / lights, the construction console, and live JSON introspection.
The nested-container spatial inventory (person to pocket to wallet).
Crash-safe logging: every log line tees to disk plus a panic hook, so a windowed crash leaves its cause in the app data logs folder.
Battery state-of-charge: the home's battery banks now charge and discharge with the day/night solar swing, with a live HUD readout (so "autonomy" is a draining number, not a static string).

Multiplayer co-presence: the client joins the relay's shared world over the existing socket, streams its position, and renders other players as moving avatars (the relay half was already done). Needs a two-player VPS test to confirm.
Multistory editor (level-aware adjacency), the structural de-risk spike, and the web accessibility modes wired into every page.
First cargo audit on the cadence (TLS advisories patched) and the release-signing pipeline verified end to end with a check-signing health gate.
Website parity: the landing became the app Mission Dashboard, the header mirrors the app nav, the service worker went network-first, plus openings as placed objects in the construction editor.
The construction editor arc: top-down plan, 3D astral camera and room grab, the three-column editor, and the door/window slide gizmo.
Live home sim, increment 1: machines spawn as ECS entities and a live power budget (solar + electrical) drives a HUD readout.
Vault-sync anti-replay; release signing activated and independently verified.
Release signing ACTIVATED: hybrid Ed25519 + Dilithium3, fail-closed updater.
Security sprint: relay rate limits, per-author object quotas, gossip-flood caps.
Signed-manifest auto-updater and signed local-build launcher.
The relay-build fix (CI had been red for 25 releases) plus retired-page cleanup.
The universal nested-expandable widget, inline item cards, and Mining and places on universal rows.
The inventory one-panel spreadsheet redesign.
The seed economy loop.
Aeroponic towers, end to end.
Homestead designs and the first working save and load.
The gameplay-loop arc: the full survival, production, and progression sandbox.
